method_atom: A network of coupled units each described by an internal phase (angle) variable, where flow between units is driven by their phase difference through a current-phase coupling relation. A special coupling element with an inverted (sign-reversed) relation imposes a fixed half-period (π) offset on that phase difference, acting as a built-in bias. This offset creates degenerate/bistable stable states in dissipationless classical loops and, in a coherent two-level element, yields a biased quantum system displaying coherent oscillations between states.
object_atom: The phase of the superconducting order parameter in superconducting circuits: two classical single-flux-quantum cells and one phase-biased qubit, with the π phase shift realized by a superconductor-ferromagnet-superconductor sandwich weak link (a Josephson π-junction) compatible with niobium-based electronics.
brief_reason: The transferable part is a fixed-π-offset element embedded in a phase-driven coupled network creating bistability or a biased coherent two-level system, while the superconducting order-parameter phase, the SFS π-junction, and the flux-quantum/qubit hardware are the domain-specific object.
